sections.paragraph:
κωφόω (s. prec. entry; ‘make blunt’ or ‘dull’: ὀδύνας κωφοῖ=‘deadens pain’ Hippocr., Liqu. 1, 1) in our lit. only pass.

① become deaf (Hippocr., Aphor. 4, 90 ed. Littré IV 524; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 175) ITr 9:1.

② be rendered speechless (Ps 38:3, 10; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 9) Hm 11:14.—DELG s.v. κωφός.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 580
